name: Ojo Guareña Natural Monument Loop
name_original: Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña — circular desde Pedrosa de Valdeporres
description: This difficult 20.2-mile touring cycling route in Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña offers varied terrain and stunning geological features.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Merindad De Valdeporres traverse a varied landscape in the northern province of Burgos, characterized by the upper basins of the Nela and Engaña rivers and the Montes del Somo. The region features expansive forests of beech, pine, and oak, alongside dramatic natural elements like the Cascada de la Mea and the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey. Karst formations and a section of the former Santander-Mediterráneo railway line, now a "Vía Verde," offer diverse terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Merindad De Valdeporres The most popular touring cycling route is Ojo Guareña Natural Monument Loop, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the renowned karst complex. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Puentedey, a moderate 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route offers views of the impressive natural arch carved by the Nela River. Local touring cyclists also love the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Pedrosa, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through the scenic Nela River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Merindad De Valdeporres is defined by ancient forests, river valleys, and unique geological formations like natural arches. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging expeditions. The routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdeporres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Merindad De Valdeporres offers a selection of over 10 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from river valleys to unique geological formations.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Merindad De Valdeporres feature varied terrain, including easily accessible paths and more demanding sections. You'll encounter expansive forests of beech, pine, and oak, riverside vegetation along the Nela and Engaña rivers, and unique karst landscapes. A notable feature is a section of the former Santander-Mediterráneo railway line, now a 'Vía Verde,' offering a relatively flat cycling experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38218088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Exit of the Ceramico Tunnel – Trail between mountains loop from Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;. This 15.4 km route is classified as easy and offers a pleasant experience through the scenic mountain landscape.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Merindad De Valdeporres has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027966028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo Guareña Natural Monument Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 32.5 km trail exploring the renowned karst complex. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrosa train station – Via verde loop from Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 41 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Puentedey&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic circular journeys around the impressive natural arch.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7011001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaLaMea&lt;/a&gt;, especially impressive after rain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Nela River, is another must-see landmark. Routes also traverse expansive forests and follow the picturesque Nela and Engaña rivers.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes often pass through charming villages and near historical sites. You can explore the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, built upon its natural rock arch. The wider Merindades region also features megalithic monuments like the dolmen of Busnela and the significant Ojo Guareña karst complex, which holds evidence of human occupation dating back thousands of years.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The touring cycling routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's ancient forests, serene river valleys, and unique geological formations like natural arches, highlighting the diverse and scenic experiences available.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its waterfalls. While cycling, you might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7011001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaLaMea&lt;/a&gt;, or other beautiful cascades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1926350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls on the Senda del Río Engaña&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3963021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aján River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Guarguero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4979424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pisas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, a unique feature for touring cyclists is a section of the former Santander-Mediterráneo railway line. This has been transformed into a 'Vía Verde' (Greenway) or Natural Path, providing an excellent, relatively flat route for cycling. It connects the Engaña Tunnel with the old Pedrosa station, offering a distinct historical cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Merindad De Valdeporres offers pleasant cycling conditions through much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly recommended for their mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, with the forests displaying beautiful colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along shaded riverside routes, though it's wise to consider morning rides to avoid the midday heat.
tips: If you have time, I advise you to take a guided tour of the cave hermitage of San Bernabe; It lasts about 45 minutes and is super curious.

The name of this small village of only 50 inhabitants means "Bridge of God". His most photographed image is the natural stone bridge created by the Nela River. On the arch the town was created. In 2022, the association The Most Beautiful Towns in Spain announced that Puentedey was added to its list.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route offers varied terrain. While predominantly on asphalt (about 32.1 km), you'll also encounter sections of unpaved roads (221 meters) and cobblestones (187 meters). The landscape features cuestas, rocky ridges, and canyons, so be prepared for mixed surfaces and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a diverse landscape where the Eurosiberian and Mediterranean biogeographical regions meet. You can expect lush forests of oaks, gall oaks, and junipers, alongside meadows and valleys. Keep an eye out for birds of prey like golden eagles, Egyptian vultures, and griffon vultures, and potentially mammals such as roe deer, wildcats, and martens.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the stunning Ojo Guareña Natural Monument, home to one of the largest cave systems in the world. A notable stop is the Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña, a unique cave hermitage with historical paintings. The charming village of Puentedey is also a highlight.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a difficult bike ride. It involves significant elevation changes, mixed terrain including unpaved and cobblestone sections, and requires good physical fitness and appropriate cycling gear. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Pedrosa de Valdeporres. You should be able to find parking within the village, close to the trailhead. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 32.5 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 9 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, not including stops for sightseeing or rest.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and natural beauty, spring and autumn are generally ideal. The weather is usually milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but always check local weather forecasts for temperature and conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña?
faqs.answer: Cycling the route itself does not typically require a permit or entrance fee. However, if you plan to visit the interior of the Ojo Guareña caves or the Hermitage of San Bernabé, guided tours are often available and may have associated fees. It's advisable to check their official websites for current information and booking.

faqs.question: Is the Monumento Natural de Ojo Guareña — circular from Pedrosa de Valdeporres route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the outdoor areas of the Natural Monument are generally accessible, specific rules for dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas to protect wildlife. If you plan to visit any indoor attractions like the Hermitage or caves, dogs are typically not allowed.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Burgos - Túnel de La Engaña), BCB Tres ríos: Por el Valle del Manzanedo, Ebro, Trifón y Nela, and Camino Olvidado (Villasante-Bimón).

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain, including asphalt, unpaved roads, and cobblestones, a sturdy touring bicycle capable of handling varied surfaces is highly recommended. Ensure your tires are suitable for both paved and unpaved sections.
